emails.subject: Need to take some time off for medical appointments
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I've been dealing with a back issue for a while now and my doctor wants me to start physical therapy plus I may need to take days off when it flares up. I'd like to request intermittent leave starting around February 15 or so. Not sure how long — my doctor said at least through the summer.

My employee ID is R-2401 and my manager is Marcus DeLuca. I'd prefer you reach me at my work email during this.

Thanks,
James Nkomo

emails.subject: Re: Need to take some time off for medical appointments
emails.body_text:
Hi James,

Thank you for reaching out about your leave. I have reviewed your eligibility and confirmed that you ARE eligible for FMLA leave under all three federal eligibility criteria as of your requested leave start date of 02/15/2026.

Attached is your official Notice of Eligibility and Rights & Responsibilities (DOL Form WH-381), which explains your rights and obligations under FMLA. Please read it carefully.

To complete your FMLA designation, I also need a medical certification from your healthcare provider. Please find attached the appropriate certification form (WH-380-E for your own condition). The completed certification is due by 03/04/2026.

If the certification is not returned by the due date, your leave may be denied. If you anticipate any difficulty obtaining the certification by the deadline, please reply to this email so we can discuss.

Case ID: LC-2026-0203

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Re: Need to take some time off for medical appointments
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Attached is the completed medical certification from my doctor. Let me know if you need anything else.

Thanks,
James

emails.subject: FMLA Leave Designated — Case LC-2026-0203
emails.body_text:
Hi James,

I have reviewed the medical certification you provided for your FMLA leave request. I am writing to confirm that your leave has been DESIGNATED as FMLA-qualifying.

Case ID: LC-2026-0203
Leave start date: 02/15/2026
Expected end date: 08/15/2026
Total FMLA time designated: up to 480 hours
Leave format: Intermittent
Certified frequency: as documented on the medical certification

Attached is your official Designation Notice (DOL Form WH-382).

Your remaining FMLA entitlement after this leave will depend on actual usage, tracked on a rolling 12-month basis.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Leave Notification — James Nkomo
emails.body_text:
Hi Marcus,

I am writing to notify you that one of your team members will be on approved leave. Please use this notification to plan workload coverage.

Employee: James Nkomo
Leave type: approved leave
Start date: 02/15/2026
Expected end date: 08/15/2026
Leave format: Intermittent
General frequency range: approximately 1-2 days per month

For all questions about this leave, please contact me directly at leaves@ridgelinegear.com. Please do NOT contact the employee about work matters related to the leave.

Please log each intermittent absence in the attendance system and notify me at leaves@ridgelinegear.com.

CONFIDENTIALITY REMINDER: medical information about your team members is confidential under federal and Colorado law. Please do not share this notification with other team members or discuss the reason for the leave with anyone.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Nkomo out today 4/7
emails.body_text:
James called in this morning, said it's for his approved leave. Full day.

— Marcus

emails.subject: Leave of absence request
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I need to request a leave of absence for a medical procedure. My doctor has scheduled it for March 16 and estimates I'll need about 8 weeks to recover.

Employee ID: R-3055
Start date: March 16, 2026
Expected return: May 11, 2026
Reason: my own medical condition
Continuous leave
My manager is Wei Zhang

Please use my work email to reach me, or my personal email hlindqvist@icloud.com if I'm not checking work email during recovery.

Thank you,
Hannah Lindqvist

emails.subject: Re: Leave of absence request
emails.body_text:
Hi Hannah,

Thank you for submitting your FMLA leave request. I have reviewed your eligibility and confirmed that you ARE eligible for FMLA leave under all three federal eligibility criteria as of your requested leave start date of 03/16/2026.

Attached is your official Notice of Eligibility and Rights & Responsibilities (DOL Form WH-381), which explains your rights and obligations under FMLA. Please read it carefully.

To complete your FMLA designation, I also need a medical certification from your healthcare provider. Please find attached the appropriate certification form (WH-380-E for your own condition). The completed certification is due by 03/28/2026.

If the certification is not returned by the due date, your leave may be denied. If you anticipate any difficulty obtaining the certification by the deadline, please reply to this email so we can discuss.

Your leave may also qualify under Colorado FAMLI, which provides paid wage replacement through the state. If you would like to file a FAMLI claim, please contact CDLE directly at claims@famli.colorado.gov. Ridgeline does not file FAMLI claims on your behalf.

Case ID: LC-2026-0287

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Re: Leave of absence request
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Here is my completed medical certification. My doctor filled it out and signed it. I also filed a FAMLI claim with CDLE like you suggested.

Thank you,
Hannah

emails.subject: FMLA Leave Designated — Case LC-2026-0287
emails.body_text:
Hi Hannah,

I have reviewed the medical certification you provided for your FMLA leave request. I am writing to confirm that your leave has been DESIGNATED as FMLA-qualifying.

Case ID: LC-2026-0287
Leave start date: 03/16/2026
Expected end date: 05/11/2026
Total FMLA time designated: 320 hours (8 weeks)
Leave format: Continuous

A fitness-for-duty certification will be required from your healthcare provider before you may return to work. I will send a reminder before your scheduled return date.

Attached is your official Designation Notice (DOL Form WH-382).

Your remaining FMLA entitlement after this leave will be approximately 160 hours in your rolling 12-month period.

Your leave also qualifies under Colorado FAMLI, and both programs will run concurrently. You will receive a separate confirmation about FAMLI coordination.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Leave Notification — Hannah Lindqvist
emails.body_text:
Hi Wei,

I am writing to notify you that one of your team members will be on approved leave. Please use this notification to plan workload coverage.

Employee: Hannah Lindqvist
Leave type: approved leave
Start date: 03/16/2026
Expected end date: 05/11/2026
Leave format: Continuous

For all questions about this leave, please contact me directly at leaves@ridgelinegear.com. Please do NOT contact the employee about work matters during their leave except in genuine operational emergency.

I will be in touch closer to the return date to coordinate.

CONFIDENTIALITY REMINDER: medical information about your team members is confidential under federal and Colorado law. Please do not share this notification with other team members or discuss the reason for the leave with anyone.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Re: FAMLI Employer Response Required — Claim FAMLI-2026-22187
emails.body_text:
To whom it may concern,

This is Ridgeline Gear Co.'s employer response for FAMLI Claim ID FAMLI-2026-22187.

Claimant: Hannah Lindqvist
Ridgeline Employee ID: R-3055
Employment Status: Active, full-time (1.0 FTE)
Leave Dates: 03/16/2026 through 05/11/2026 (expected)
FMLA Running Concurrently: Yes — Ridgeline Case ID LC-2026-0287

Please contact me if additional information is needed.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: FAMLI Concurrent Designation — Case LC-2026-0287
emails.body_text:
Hi Hannah,

I am writing to confirm that I have received the notice from CDLE about your Colorado FAMLI claim. I have submitted Ridgeline's Employer Response and confirmed the following:

FAMLI Claim ID: FAMLI-2026-22187
Case ID at Ridgeline: LC-2026-0287
Concurrent FMLA designation: Yes

Because your leave qualifies under both FMLA and FAMLI, both programs will run concurrently.

Wage replacement under FAMLI is paid by the State of Colorado, NOT by Ridgeline. All questions about your FAMLI wage replacement amount, payment timing, and tax treatment should be directed to CDLE directly at claims@famli.colorado.gov. I cannot answer those questions.

If you have questions about Ridgeline's role in coordinating your leave, please contact me directly. If you have questions about pay or benefits, please contact our Payroll Lead, Teresa Vang, at teresa.vang@ridgelinegear.com.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Jury duty next week
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I got a jury summons for Larimer County District Court starting Monday April 6. Attached is a scan of the summons. Not sure how long it'll go — could be just a day or the whole week.

Marcus DeLuca
R-3112

emails.subject: Re: Jury duty next week
emails.body_text:
Hi Marcus,

Thank you for letting me know about your jury duty and for providing the summons. I've logged this in our system.

Case ID: LC-2026-0320
Leave start date: 04/06/2026
Leave type: Jury Duty

You are covered for the full length of jury service. Please let me know when the service ends so I can update your case and coordinate with Payroll.

I've notified Sandra Ellison that you'll be out starting Monday.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Leave Notification — Marcus DeLuca
emails.body_text:

I am writing to notify you that one of your team members will be on approved leave. Please use this notification to plan workload coverage.

Employee: Marcus DeLuca
Leave type: jury duty
Start date: 04/06/2026
Expected end date: to be confirmed (estimated up to 1 week)
Leave format: Continuous

For all questions about this leave, please contact me directly at leaves@ridgelinegear.com.

CONFIDENTIALITY REMINDER: Please do not share this notification with other team members.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Bereavement leave — my father passed away
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I'm writing to let you know that my father passed away last night. I need to take bereavement leave starting today through Monday. My employee ID is R-3200 and my manager is Rachel Thornton.

Kevin O'Malley

emails.subject: Re: Bereavement leave — my father passed away
emails.body_text:
Hi Kevin,

I am very sorry for your loss. I want to confirm that I have received your bereavement leave request and have logged the following:

Case ID: LC-2026-0305
Leave start date: 03/28/2026
Leave end date: 03/30/2026
Total bereavement leave: 3 days

Your manager has been notified that you will be out, with no personal details disclosed.

Per Ridgeline policy, please provide documentation (an obituary, death certificate, or funeral program) within 30 calendar days.

If you need additional time beyond your bereavement allowance, please let me know and we can discuss options.

Please take care of yourself during this difficult time.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Re: Bereavement leave — my father passed away
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Attached is the obituary for my father. Thank you for your kind words.

Kevin

emails.subject: Paternity leave request
emails.body_text:
Hi,

My wife is expecting our first child in early January. I'd like to request leave for bonding starting around January 12 for 8 weeks. My employee ID is R-2300 and my manager is Amy Sandoval.

Thanks,
Darnell Washington

emails.subject: FMLA Leave Designated — Case LC-2026-0101
emails.body_text:
Hi Darnell,

Congratulations on the upcoming arrival! I have reviewed your eligibility and confirmed that you ARE eligible for FMLA leave. Your leave has been DESIGNATED as FMLA-qualifying.

Case ID: LC-2026-0101
Leave start date: 01/12/2026
Expected end date: 03/06/2026
Total FMLA time designated: 320 hours (8 weeks)
Leave format: Continuous

No medical certification is required for bonding leave.

Your leave also qualifies under Colorado FAMLI. If you have not already, you may file a FAMLI claim with CDLE at claims@famli.colorado.gov for paid wage replacement.

Your remaining FMLA entitlement after this leave will be approximately 160 hours in your rolling 12-month period.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Leave request — need to care for my mom
emails.body_text:
Hi,

My mother was hospitalized this week and the doctors say she'll need ongoing care for the next several weeks. I need to take leave starting Monday October 6 to be with her. I'm not sure exactly how long — maybe 3 to 4 weeks.

My employee ID is R-2108, manager is Marcus DeLuca. Please reach me on my work email.

Thanks,
Tomás Guerrero

emails.subject: Continuing intermittent leave for 2026
emails.body_text:
Hi,

My intermittent FMLA case (LC-2025-0331) ends December 31 but I still need intermittent leave for the same condition going into 2026. My doctor renewed the certification on 12/20. Should I open a new case or does the old one continue?

Thanks,
Sarah Chen
R-2044

emails.subject: Re: Continuing intermittent leave for 2026
emails.body_text:
Hi Sarah,

Thank you for letting me know and for getting the certification renewed. I've opened a new case for the continuation:

Case ID: LC-2026-0058
Leave start date: 01/01/2026
Expected end date: 03/31/2026
Leave format: Intermittent

Your renewed certification is on file, so no additional paperwork is needed right now. Your FMLA designation carries over to the new case.

As a reminder, your rolling 12-month FMLA balance is calculated from all usage in the prior 12 months, so hours used under your previous cases still count toward your total.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Sick today and tomorrow
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I woke up feeling really unwell this morning. I'm going to be out today and probably tomorrow as well. Wanted to let you know.

Aisha Robinson, R-3278
My manager is Jennifer Kowalski.

emails.subject: Re: Sick today and tomorrow
emails.body_text:
Hi Aisha,

I'm sorry to hear you're not feeling well. I've logged your absence under HFWA sick leave:

Case ID: LC-2025-0275
Dates: 08/18/2025 – 08/19/2025
Hours: 12 (based on your regular 6-hour workday)

No documentation is needed since this is fewer than 4 consecutive workdays. Your manager has been notified of the dates only.

Feel better soon.

Best regards,
Ridgeline Gear Co. — Leave Coordinator
leaves@ridgelinegear.com

emails.subject: Leave tracker access for new LC
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Just a reminder that the shared drive permissions for the medical files folder were updated last week. If you have any trouble accessing files, let Samir know and he'll fix it.

Also, the Q2 quarterly audit is coming up April 15. I'll be pulling cases next week so please make sure all your recent closures have complete case notes.

emails.subject: Need to take leave for surgery
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I have a medical condition that my doctor says requires surgery. The procedure is scheduled for April 20 and my doctor estimates I will need about 4 weeks to recover, so I would be out from April 20 through May 15.

My employee ID is R-2317 and my manager is Amy Sandoval. While I am out please reach me at my personal email elenavee@gmail.com since I will not be checking work email during recovery.

Please let me know what I need to do.

Thank you,
Elena Vasquez
